PLAINTIFFS JOSEPH GARZA, MARY GARZA'S MOTION FOR MONETARY AND NONMONETARY SANCTIONS PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 2030.300(E), C.C.P. 2023.030 (A) AND (D)(2) ET SEQ., C.C.P. 2031.310(E), AND C.C.P. 2031.320(C)
Argued and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Plaintiffs' motion for monetary and nonmonetary sanctions is granted in part and denied in part. The request for monetary sanction of $25,000 per day until defendant answers the post-judgment interrogatories is denied on the grounds that there is no California authority authorizing such sanctions. The request for "disentitlement" sanctions is granted in part and denied in part. Defendant is precluded from submitting any opposition to any motion seeking the turnover of personal and/or real property owed by it to this court. The request for disentitlement sanctions with regard to the turning over of property to a Pennsylvania court is denied on the grounds that there is no California authority authorizing such sanctions and the request would be an improper interference with the rules and proceeding of a court located in another state. RULING:
Plaintiffs JOSEPH GARZA & MARY GARZA's motion for assignment of rights, restraining order and turnover order. Motion was argued and tentative ruling adopted and amplified as follows: Plaintiffs' motion for assignment of rights, restraining order and turnover order is denied without prejudice to re-filing a more narrowly tailored motion which specifies with considerably greater precision the rights sought to be assigned, the conduct sought to be restrained and the property sought to be turned over. The motion is now so broad that it is unclear what is covered by it and thus any order resulting from the motion is likely to be too vague to be enforced. Because the motion is so non-specific, the court cannot evaluate whether granting any or all of the motion violates ACL's due process rights. If plaintiffs are precluded from filing a more narrowly tailored motion by nonresponsive or evasive answers to the post-judgment discovery, the Court will reconsider the motion as framed now. The Court further finds that ACL's assertion that there's been insufficient or improper service or notice has been waived to deal with the merits of the motion. Ms Gambino is to prepare an order for the Court's signature.
A status conference was held. Mary Ellen Gambino was sworn and examined. Exhibit A - Memorandum of Garnishee prepared by Wilson and Elser was marked for identification. The Court ordered plaintiff's counsel to file the document via LexisNexis. The parties are to meet and confer and Court will set a further status conference if needed.
